seo.title: Building Voice Agents with Agno + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build voice agents with Agno + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Building voice agents with Agno enables real-time conversational AI through its lightweight, model-agnostic framework that handles multi-modal inputs seamlessly. However, the high-stakes nature of voice interactions—demanding sub-second latency, coherent dialogue, and safety—makes reliability critical. Vex provides essential guardrails to verify response appropriateness, latency budgets, conversation coherence, and safety filtering, preventing costly failures in production.

architectureOverview.heading: Voice Agents Architecture with Agno
architectureOverview.description:
Voice agents with Agno follow a real-time pipeline: audio input is transcribed via speech-to-text (e.g., Whisper or Deepgram), processed by an Agno agent for LLM reasoning with tools and memory, then synthesized back to speech using text-to-speech (e.g., ElevenLabs or PlayHT). Agno's agent.run() orchestrates this with session memory for context-aware responses. The architecture prioritizes low-latency components and streaming where possible to meet sub-second E2E requirements.

Key components include Agno's Agent class configured with voice-specific tools (transcription, TTS), RAG for knowledge retrieval, and structured output schemas for parsing. Session state tracks conversation history to maintain coherence across turns. Vex wraps the agent.run() call to enforce SLAs on latency, safety, and output quality before TTS rendering.

This setup scales to production with WebSocket endpoints for bidirectional streaming, ensuring natural conversational flow while mitigating risks like hallucinated memory or unsafe responses.

risks.name: Multi-modal agent misinterpreting audio inputs
risks.description: STT transcription errors lead to LLM reasoning on garbled text, producing irrelevant or unsafe responses
risks.mitigation: Vex verifies transcription confidence scores and response appropriateness before TTS

risks.name: Agentic session memory recalling fabricated interactions
risks.description: Hallucinated conversation history causes context drift and incoherent dialogue flow
risks.mitigation: Vex checks conversation coherence against session state and flags memory fabrications

risks.name: Latency budget violations
risks.description: E2E response exceeding 500ms threshold breaks real-time conversational UX
risks.mitigation: Vex enforces strict latency SLAs with automatic fallbacks to cached responses

risks.name: Safety filter bypasses
risks.description: LLM generates harmful content that slips through basic moderation checks
risks.mitigation: Vex applies multi-layer safety verification including toxicity scoring and intent classification

implementation.title: Basic Agno Voice Agent Setup
implementation.description: Initialize Agno agent with voice tools and session memory for conversational context
implementation.code:
from agno.agent import Agent
from agno.tools import tool

@tool
def transcribe_audio(audio_bytes: bytes) -> str:
    """Transcribe audio to text using Whisper API"""
    # Implementation with openai/whisper
    pass

voice_agent = Agent(
    model="gpt-4o-mini",
    tools=[transcribe_audio],
    show_tool_calls=True,
    add_base_tools=False,
    show_tool_outputs=True,
    add_memory=True,
    description="Real-time voice conversation agent"
)

implementation.title: Real-time Streaming Pipeline
implementation.description: WebSocket handler for continuous audio input → agent → TTS output
implementation.code:
import asyncio
import websockets
from agno import Agent

async def voice_websocket(websocket, path):
    agent = Agent.from_config('voice-agent.yaml')
    
    async for message in websocket:
        # Stream audio → STT → agent reasoning → TTS
        transcript = await stt_pipeline(message)
        response = await agent.run(transcript)
        audio_out = await tts_pipeline(response)
        await websocket.send(audio_out)

start_server = websockets.serve(voice_websocket, "0.0.0.0", 8765)
asyncio.get_event_loop().run_until_complete(start_server)

implementation.title: Latency-Monitored Agent Execution
implementation.description: Wrap agent.run() with timing decorators for sub-second SLA enforcement
implementation.code:
import time
from agno.agent import Agent

class VoiceAgent:
    def __init__(self):
        self.agent = Agent(model='gpt-4o-mini', add_memory=True)
    
    async def run_with_latency_check(self, input_text: str, max_latency=0.5):
        start_time = time.time()
        response = await self.agent.run(input_text)
        latency = time.time() - start_time
        
        if latency > max_latency:
            raise TimeoutError(f"Exceeded {max_latency}s latency budget")
        
        return response

implementation.title: Structured Voice Response Schema
implementation.description: Enforce JSON output for safe TTS rendering with safety fields
implementation.code:
from pydantic import BaseModel, Field
from agno import Agent

class VoiceResponse(BaseModel):
    text: str = Field(..., description="TTS text")
    safety_score: float = Field(..., ge=0.0, le=1.0)
    intent: str = Field(..., pattern="^(safe|warning|blocked)$")
    latency_ms: int

agent = Agent(
    model="gpt-4o-mini",
    response_format=VoiceResponse,
    instructions="Always return structured VoiceResponse with safety analysis"
)

vexIntegration.title: Vex Decorator for agent.run()
vexIntegration.description: Wrap Agno's agent.run() with vex.verify() for comprehensive guardrails
vexIntegration.code:
from vex import verify
from agno.agent import Agent

@verify(
    guards=[
        "response_appropriateness",
        "latency_budget=500ms",
        "conversation_coherence",
        "safety_filtering"
    ]
)
async def safe_voice_agent_run(agent: Agent, input_text: str):
    """Verified voice agent execution"""
    return await agent.run(input_text)

# Usage
voice_agent = Agent(model="gpt-4o-mini", add_memory=True)
response = await safe_voice_agent_run(voice_agent, "Hello, how can I help?")

vexIntegration.title: Custom Vex Guard for Voice Safety
vexIntegration.description: Define voice-specific safety checks integrated with Agno session state

vexIntegration.title: Latency + Safety Pipeline with Fallbacks
vexIntegration.description: Production pipeline with Vex fallbacks for latency/safety violations
vexIntegration.code:
from vex import verify, Fallback
from agno.agent import Agent

@verify(
    guards=["latency_budget=450ms", "safety_filtering"],
    fallback=Fallback(
        strategy="greeting",
        response="One moment please..."
    )
)
async def production_voice_pipeline(audio_input: bytes, session_id: str):
    transcript = await stt_transcribe(audio_input)
    agent = Agent.load(session_id)  # Load session memory
    response = await agent.run(transcript)
    return await tts_synthesize(response)

# Handles verification failures gracefully
result = await production_voice_pipeline(audio_bytes, "user_123")

cta.heading: Secure Your Agno Voice Agents
cta.description: Install Vex today with `pip install agno vex-sdk` and wrap your agent.run() calls to achieve production-grade reliability.
